docs.title: Reply-To Schemes
docs.text:
When a job is created with reply_to, the server delivers the result asynchronously when the job completes.

Scheme Example Behavior mattermost://{channel-id} mattermost://your-channel-id-here POST to Mattermost channel https://... Any webhook URL HTTP POST with JSON payload openclaw://{agent} openclaw://avery POST to OpenClaw agent harness

Webhook payload: 

{
  "job_id": "abc123",
  "status": "completed",
  "result": "...",
  "agent": "innie"
}

docs.title: Timeouts
docs.text: Variable Default Description INNIE_SYNC_TIMEOUT 1800 Sync job max seconds INNIE_ASYNC_TIMEOUT 7200 Async job max seconds

docs.title: CLI Reference
docs.text: All commands: innie <command> [OPTIONS]

docs.title: innie init
docs.text:
Set up ~/.innie/, run the setup wizard, install hooks, create default agent.

innie init                  # Interactive wizard
innie init --local          # No Docker, keyword search only
innie init -y               # Accept all defaults non-interactively
innie init --local -y       # Silent local-only setup
Option Default Description --local false Skip Docker/embeddings, keyword search only -y, --yes false Non-interactive, accept all defaults

docs.title: innie create <name>
docs.text:
Create a new agent with scaffolded directories.

innie create mybot
innie create mybot --role "Research Assistant"
innie create mybot --soul "I am a research assistant..."

Creates: agents/<name>/SOUL.md, CONTEXT.md, profile.yaml, HEARTBEAT.md, data/, state/, skills/

docs.title: innie list
docs.text:
List all agents with role and stats.

innie list

docs.title: innie delete <name>
docs.text:
Archive and remove an agent.

innie delete mybot
innie delete mybot --force   # Skip confirmation

docs.title: innie switch <name>
docs.text:
Set the active agent (writes defaults.agent in config.toml).

innie switch mybot

docs.title: innie status
docs.text:
Show current agent status, hook health, index stats.

innie status
innie status --agent mybot

docs.title: Memory Commands
docs.text: Live in-session knowledge base operations. No need to wait for heartbeat — writes take effect immediately.

docs.title: innie memory store <type> <title> <content>
docs.text:
Write a learning, decision, or project update directly to the knowledge base.

# Store a learning
innie memory store learning "sqlite-vec requires integer chunk_id" \
  "vec0 tables require the rowid column to be explicitly declared INTEGER PRIMARY KEY" \
  --category tools --confidence high

# Store a decision
innie memory store decision "Use RRF for hybrid search fusion" \
  "Chose Reciprocal Rank Fusion over linear interpolation — simpler, no tuning required" \
  --project innie-engine

# Append a project update
innie memory store project "innie-engine" "Completed Phase 1 live memory management"
Option Default Description --category tools Learning category: debugging | patterns | tools | infrastructure | processes --confidence medium high | medium | low --project general Project name (for decision type)

Writes to data/, immediately indexes for search, appends to data/memory-ops.jsonl.

docs.title: innie memory forget <path> <reason>
docs.text:
Mark a knowledge base entry as superseded. Does not delete — marks with superseded: true frontmatter.

innie memory forget learnings/tools/2026-03-01-foo.md "API changed in v0.6 — use bar() instead"

Path is relative to data/.

docs.title: innie memory ops [--since N]
docs.text:
Show recent memory operations from this session.

innie memory ops             # Last 8 hours (default)
innie memory ops --since 24  # Last 24 hours

docs.title: innie memory quality [--days N]
docs.text:
Show memory quality stats: top retrieved files, never-retrieved learnings, confidence distribution, and decay candidates.

innie memory quality          # Last 7 days (default)
innie memory quality --days 30

Output panels: - Top Retrieved — files most frequently surfaced in context injection - Learnings Never Retrieved — data/learnings/ files with zero hits (up to 15) - Confidence Distribution — bar chart of high/medium/low/none across all files - Decay candidates — low-confidence learnings never retrieved (act with innie memory forget)

docs.title: innie context
docs.text:
View and manage CONTEXT.md working memory.

# Print current CONTEXT.md
innie context

# Add an open item (takes effect next session)
innie context add "- Wire TailSweep into Mercury main app"

# Remove an open item by substring match (takes effect next session)
innie context remove "Wire TailSweep"

# Dedup and trim Open Items via LLM (shows diff, prompts before writing)
innie context compress
innie context compress --apply   # Write directly without confirmation

# Load and print a full knowledge base file (for index-only mode)
innie context load learnings/tools/2026-03-01-slug.md

Note: all context subcommands write to disk immediately but the live session context is a frozen snapshot — changes appear at next session start.

Index-only mode: When data/ exceeds context.index_threshold files (default: 200), <memory-context> switches to path+score references only. innie context load is the on-demand fetch for full file content.

docs.title: innie ls [path]
docs.text:
Browse the knowledge base directory structure.

# Show top-level directories with file counts
innie ls

# List files in a specific subdirectory
innie ls learnings/tools
innie ls projects

docs.title: innie search <query>
docs.text:
Search the knowledge base.

innie search "JWT refresh tokens"
innie search "what did we decide about caching" --mode hybrid
innie search "docker configuration" --mode keyword
innie search "deployment patterns" --limit 10
Option Default Description --mode hybrid hybrid | keyword | semantic --limit 5 Number of results --agent active agent Agent to search

docs.title: innie index
docs.text:
Build or refresh the semantic index.

innie index                 # Full rebuild
innie index --changed       # Only re-index changed files
innie index --agent mybot

docs.title: innie heartbeat run
docs.text:
Run the heartbeat pipeline (collect → extract → route).

innie heartbeat run
innie heartbeat run --dry-run      # Preview without writing
innie heartbeat run --agent mybot

docs.title: innie heartbeat status
docs.text:
Show when heartbeat last ran and what's pending.

innie heartbeat status

docs.title: innie backend list
docs.text:
List all detected AI coding assistant backends.

innie backend list

docs.title: innie backend install
docs.text:
Install innie hooks into the detected backend.

innie backend install
innie backend install --backend claude-code

docs.title: innie backend uninstall
docs.text:
Remove all innie hooks from all backends.

innie backend uninstall

docs.title: innie backend check
docs.text:
Verify hook installation status.

innie backend check

docs.title: innie skill list
docs.text:
List all available skills (built-in + agent custom skills).

innie skill list
innie skill list --agent mybot

docs.title: innie skill run <name>
docs.text:
Run a built-in skill.

innie skill run daily --args '{"summary": "Shipped auth feature"}'
innie skill run learn --args '{"category": "patterns", "title": "RRF", "content": "..."}'
innie skill run inbox --args '{"content": "Remember to update docs"}'

docs.title: Session Commands
docs.text: Search across indexed session content. Sessions are indexed automatically at heartbeat time.

docs.title: innie session list [--days N] [--limit N]
docs.text:
List recently indexed sessions with start time, duration, and source.

innie session list              # Last 30 days (default)
innie session list --days 7

docs.title: innie session search "query" [--limit N]
docs.text:
FTS keyword search across session transcripts with highlighted excerpts. Shows source file path when available.

innie session search "Traefik certificate"
innie session search "docker compose port mapping" --limit 10

docs.title: innie session read <session-id>
docs.text:
Read full session content. Reads the raw source JSONL if still on disk; falls back to the cached transcript from the index.

innie session read abc123             # Full or prefix match
innie session read abc123 --raw       # Print raw JSONL lines

docs.title: innie trace list
docs.text:
List recent trace sessions with duration, cost, and token counts.

innie trace list                    # Last 20 sessions
innie trace list --agent mybot      # Filter by agent
innie trace list --days 3           # Last 3 days only
innie trace list --limit 50         # More results
Option Default Description --agent all Filter by agent name --days 7 How many days back --limit 20 Max sessions to show

docs.title: innie trace show <session_id>
docs.text:
Show a session's detail with all tool spans.

innie trace show abc123             # Full session ID
innie trace show abc                # Prefix match works too

docs.title: innie trace stats
docs.text:
Show aggregate trace statistics — total cost, token usage, tool breakdown, daily activity.

innie trace stats                   # Last 30 days
innie trace stats --agent mybot     # Filter by agent
innie trace stats --days 7          # Last week only

docs.title: innie fleet start
docs.text:
Start the fleet gateway.

innie fleet start
innie fleet start --port 8020
innie fleet start --host 127.0.0.1 --config ./fleet.yaml
innie fleet start --reload      # Dev mode

docs.title: innie fleet agents
docs.text:
Show all agents in the fleet with health status.

innie fleet agents

docs.title: innie fleet stats
docs.text:
Show fleet-wide statistics.

innie fleet stats

docs.title: innie serve
docs.text:
Start the jobs API and memory server.

innie serve
innie serve --port 8013 --host 0.0.0.0
innie serve --reload        # Dev mode

docs.title: innie decay
docs.text:
Run memory decay (archive old context, compress sessions, clean index).

innie decay
innie decay --dry-run
innie decay --context-days 30 --session-days 90

docs.title: innie doctor
docs.text:
Run diagnostics — check hooks, index health, config validity.

innie doctor

docs.title: innie alias add <name>
docs.text:
Add a shell alias to ~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc.

innie alias add innie              # Creates: alias innie='INNIE_AGENT=innie claude'
innie alias add mybot              # Creates: alias mybot='INNIE_AGENT=mybot claude'

docs.title: innie alias remove <name>
docs.text:
Remove a previously installed shell alias.

innie alias remove innie
innie alias remove mybot

docs.title: innie migrate
docs.text:
Import from existing agent-harness, openclaw, or generic directories.

innie migrate --dry-run                    # Preview
innie migrate                              # Auto-detect and import
innie migrate --source /path/to/dir        # Specific directory
innie migrate --agent mybot               # Import to specific agent
innie migrate --all                        # Import all detected setups

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Description Default INNIE_HOME Root data directory ~/.innie INNIE_AGENT Active agent name from config.toml INNIE_FLEET_CONFIG Fleet config path ~/.innie/fleet.yaml INNIE_SYNC_TIMEOUT Sync job timeout (seconds) 1800 INNIE_ASYNC_TIMEOUT Async job timeout (seconds) 7200

docs.title: Configuration Reference
docs.text:
Config file: ~/.innie/config.toml

Generated by innie init. All values have defaults and the file is not required.

docs.title: Full Config Reference
docs.text:
# ~/.innie/config.toml

[user]
name = "Josh"               # Your name (injected into session context)
timezone = "America/Chicago"

[defaults]
agent = "innie"             # Active agent when INNIE_AGENT not set

# ── Embedding ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[embedding]
provider = "docker"         # docker | external | none
model = "bge-base-en"       # Model name passed to embedding API

[embedding.docker]
url = "http://localhost:8766"  # Local embedding service (docker compose up)

[embedding.external]
# url = "https://api.openai.com/v1"
# api_key_env = "OPENAI_API_KEY"    # Env var name (never hardcode keys)
# model = "text-embedding-3-small"

# ── Heartbeat ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[heartbeat]
enabled = false             # Enable automatic scheduled heartbeat
interval = "30m"            # Cron-style or duration string
provider = "auto"           # auto | anthropic | external
model = "auto"              # LLM model name, or "auto" to pick default per provider
external_url = ""           # OpenAI-compatible endpoint (required when provider = "external")
collect_git = true          # Include git log/diff in collection
collect_sessions = true     # Include session logs in collection
auto_update = false         # Reinstall innie-engine at start of each heartbeat

# ── Index ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[index]
chunk_words = 300           # Words per text chunk
chunk_overlap = 60          # Overlap words between chunks (20%)
chunk_markdown_aware = true # Split on markdown headers before word-windowing

# ── Search ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[search]
query_expansion = false     # Generate alt query phrasing via LLM (adds ~1-2s)
expansion_model = "auto"    # "auto" = uses heartbeat.model and heartbeat.external_url

# ── Context ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[context]
max_tokens = 2000           # Max characters injected at session start

# ── Git ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[git]
auto_commit = false         # Auto git commit data/ after heartbeat
auto_push = false           # Auto git push after commit (requires remote)

docs.title: [user]
docs.text: Key Type Default Description name string "" Your name, injected into session context timezone string "UTC" Your timezone (e.g., "America/Chicago")

docs.title: [defaults]
docs.text: Key Type Default Description agent string "innie" Default active agent when INNIE_AGENT not set

docs.title: [embedding]
docs.text:
Key Type Default Description provider string "docker" docker | external | none model string "bge-base-en" Embedding model name

provider = "docker" — runs local bge-base-en via Docker on port 8766. Zero cost, no API key. Run docker compose up -d embeddings first.

provider = "external" — any OpenAI-compatible embedding endpoint.

provider = "none" — keyword-only search. No vectors, no Docker required.

docs.title: [embedding.docker]
docs.text: Key Type Default Description url string "http://localhost:8766" Embedding service URL

docs.title: [embedding.external]
docs.text: Key Type Default Description url string — OpenAI-compatible embeddings endpoint api_key_env string — Env var name containing API key model string — Model name to request

docs.title: [heartbeat]
docs.text:
Key Type Default Description enabled bool false Enable automatic heartbeat on schedule interval string "30m" How often to run (duration or cron) provider string "auto" auto | anthropic | external model string "auto" LLM model name, or "auto" to pick default per provider external_url string "" OpenAI-compatible endpoint (required when provider = "external") collect_git bool true Include git log/diff in Phase 1 collect_sessions bool true Include session logs in Phase 1 auto_update bool false Reinstall innie-engine from PyPI (or local source if editable) at the start of each heartbeat

provider = "auto" — uses external if external_url is set, otherwise falls back to anthropic. Existing installs with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY continue to work unchanged.

provider = "external" — routes to any OpenAI-compatible /chat/completions endpoint. Works with vLLM, Ollama (in OpenAI compat mode), LM Studio, llama.cpp server, etc. No API key required. Set external_url and model explicitly.

provider = "anthropic" — Anthropic Messages API. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY must be set in the environment. model = "auto" resolves to claude-haiku-4-5-20251001.

docs.title: [index]
docs.text:
Key Type Default Description chunk_words int 300 Target words per chunk chunk_overlap int 60 Overlap words between adjacent chunks (20%) chunk_markdown_aware bool true Split on ##/### headers before word-windowing

Tuning: Smaller chunks = more precise retrieval but less context per result. 300/60 targets the useful embedding range for BGE-base-en (~225 tokens). Set chunk_markdown_aware = false to restore pure word-window behavior.

docs.title: [search]
docs.text:
Key Type Default Description query_expansion bool false Generate one alt phrasing via LLM before searching expansion_model string "auto" "auto" uses heartbeat.model and heartbeat.external_url

query_expansion: Bridges vocabulary mismatch (e.g., searching "login" when content says "authentication"). Adds ~1-2s latency. Silent no-op if the LLM endpoint is unavailable.

docs.title: [context]
docs.text: Key Type Default Description max_tokens int 2000 Max characters from search results injected at session start

docs.title: [git]
docs.text:
Key Type Default Description auto_commit bool false git add -A && git commit in data/ after each heartbeat auto_push bool false git push after commit (requires remote configured)

git.auto_push

Only enable auto_push if you have a remote configured and appropriate credentials. innie does not manage git credentials.

docs.title: Dot-Notation Access
docs.text:
Config values can be read in code with dot notation:

from innie.core.config import get

provider = get("embedding.provider", "docker")
model = get("heartbeat.model", "auto")
commit = get("git.auto_commit", False)

docs.title: Multiple Agents, Single Config
docs.text:
All agents share the same config.toml. Agent-specific behavior is controlled by:

  1. INNIE_AGENT env var or defaults.agent in config — selects active agent
  2. HEARTBEAT.md — per-agent extraction instructions
  3. SOUL.md — per-agent identity
  4. Per-command --agent flag

docs.title: Built-in Skills
docs.text: Skills are structured knowledge entry commands. They create well-formatted files in the right location of your knowledge base.

docs.title: daily — Daily Journal Entry
docs.text:
Creates or appends to the daily journal file for today.

Output: data/journal/YYYY/MM/DD.md

builtins.daily(
    summary: str,                        # required
    highlights: list[str] | None = None, # optional bullet points
    blockers: list[str] | None = None,   # optional blockers
    agent: str | None = None,
)

CLI: 

innie skill run daily --args '{
  "summary": "Shipped the auth feature",
  "highlights": ["JWT refresh works", "Rate limiting done"],
  "blockers": ["Deploy blocked by staging env"]
}'

Output format: 

## 14:30

Shipped the auth feature

**Highlights:**
- JWT refresh works
- Rate limiting done

**Blockers:**
- Deploy blocked by staging env

docs.title: learn — Learning Entry
docs.text:
Saves a categorized insight to the learnings directory.

Output: data/learnings/{category}/YYYY-MM-DD-{slug}.md

builtins.learn(
    title: str,
    content: str,
    category: str = "patterns",   # debugging|patterns|tools|infrastructure|processes
    tags: list[str] | None = None,
    agent: str | None = None,
)

CLI: 

innie skill run learn --args '{
  "title": "RRF Search Fusion",
  "content": "Reciprocal Rank Fusion combines keyword and vector search results by rank position. Score = 1/(k+rank) summed across lists. k=60 is the standard constant.",
  "category": "patterns",
  "tags": ["search", "information-retrieval"]
}'

Categories: - debugging — debugging techniques, root cause patterns - patterns — architectural patterns, design decisions - tools — how specific tools work - infrastructure — ops, deployment, config patterns - processes — workflows, team processes

docs.title: meeting — Meeting Notes
docs.text:
Creates structured meeting notes.

Output: data/meetings/YYYY-MM-DD-{slug}.md

builtins.meeting(
    title: str,
    attendees: list[str],
    notes: str,
    action_items: list[str] | None = None,
    decisions: list[str] | None = None,
    agent: str | None = None,
)

CLI: 

innie skill run meeting --args '{
  "title": "Auth System Design Review",
  "attendees": ["alice", "bob"],
  "notes": "Reviewed JWT vs session tokens. Decided on JWT with 15m expiry.",
  "action_items": ["Alice to implement refresh endpoint", "Bob to update docs"],
  "decisions": ["Use JWT, 15min expiry", "Refresh tokens in Redis"]
}'

docs.title: contact — Contact Profile
docs.text:
Creates or appends to a contact profile.

Output: data/people/{slug}.md

builtins.contact(
    name: str,
    role: str = "",
    notes: str = "",
    contact_info: dict[str, str] | None = None,
    agent: str | None = None,
)

CLI: 

innie skill run contact --args '{
  "name": "Alice Chen",
  "role": "Senior Engineer",
  "notes": "Expert in distributed systems. Prefers async communication.",
  "contact_info": {"email": "alice@example.com", "github": "alicechen"}
}'

docs.title: inbox — Inbox Capture
docs.text:
Appends to an append-only inbox file. Quick capture without categorization.

Output: data/inbox/inbox.md

builtins.inbox(
    content: str,
    source: str = "manual",
    agent: str | None = None,
)

CLI: 

innie skill run inbox --args '{"content": "Look into bloom filters for dedup"}'
innie skill run inbox --args '{"content": "Ask Alice about the Redis config", "source": "meeting"}'

The inbox is intentionally unstructured. Process it periodically into proper learnings/decisions/tasks.

docs.title: adr — Architecture Decision Record
docs.text:
Creates a formatted ADR (Architecture Decision Record).

Output: data/decisions/NNNN-{slug}.md

The file number is automatically determined (next available NNNN).

builtins.adr(
    title: str,
    context: str,
    decision: str,
    alternatives: list[str] | None = None,
    consequences: list[str] | None = None,
    status: str = "accepted",   # accepted|proposed|deprecated|superseded
    agent: str | None = None,
)

CLI: 

innie skill run adr --args '{
  "title": "Use SQLite for local storage",
  "context": "Need embedded storage with full-text search. Considered PostgreSQL, DuckDB, and SQLite.",
  "decision": "Use SQLite with FTS5 extension. It is zero-dependency, widely available, and FTS5 is built-in.",
  "alternatives": ["PostgreSQL: requires server process", "DuckDB: excellent analytics but overkill for our use case"],
  "consequences": ["Pro: zero setup, Pro: FTS5 built-in, Con: limited concurrent writes"],
  "status": "accepted"
}'

Output format: 

# NNNN — Use SQLite for local storage

*Status: accepted | Date: 2026-03-02*

## Context

Need embedded storage with full-text search...

## Decision

Use SQLite with FTS5 extension...

## Alternatives Considered

- PostgreSQL: requires server process
- DuckDB: excellent analytics but overkill

## Consequences

- Pro: zero setup
- Pro: FTS5 built-in
- Con: limited concurrent writes

docs.title: Custom Skills
docs.text:
Create agent-specific skills by adding a SKILL.md to agents/<name>/skills/<skill-name>/:

agents/innie/skills/daily-standup/SKILL.md

The SKILL.md contains the skill's instructions/template. List custom skills:

innie skill list --agent innie

Custom skills appear in the list alongside built-ins but are invoked as slash commands inside your AI assistant session rather than via innie skill run.
